title: dysoxylum rufescens subsp. dzumacense
intro: Dysoxylum rufescens subsp. dzumacense is a species of plant in the family Meliaceae, commonly known as the Dzumac Dysoxylum, which is a type of evergreen tree native to the Philippines. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark, which has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues. The bark of Dysoxylum rufescens subsp. dzumacense contains flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and protecting against cell damage. In herbal preparations, the bark of this plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
